title: Chewy Q1 Earnings Preview: Here's What You'll Want to Know (2021)
text: Chewy was set to report first-quarter 2021 earnings on Thursday, June 10. Wall Street analysts forecast revenue of $2.13 billion (31% year-over-year growth) and earnings per share loss of $0.03. Expected gross margin was 25.7% and adjusted EBITDA estimated at $32.3M. Chewy was coming off an incredible 2020, and while many businesses were devastated by the coronavirus pandemic, Chewy's exclusively online business model was made to thrive. Active customers was a major key metric as the world recovers from COVID-19, since investor worry was that Chewy may lose some of the newly acquired customers as economies reopen. The article notes that management reported adding 5.7 million net active customers in 2020, bringing the total to 19.2 million by year-end. Q4 2020 marked the company's first profitable quarter with a 1% net margin. Analysts anticipated a return to losses in Q1, though the article notes surprising profitability is possible. Proprietary brand sales mix will significantly influence margins. Chewy trades at a 3.4 forward price-to-sales ratio, down substantially from January's 6+ P/S ratio, suggesting potential overreaction to reopening concerns.
